person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Armoirie de la famille du Caylar :
D'or à trois bandes de gueules au chef d'or chargé d'un lion issant de sable soutenu d'une divise d'or chargée de trois trèfles de sable.
Relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Mangas

geni.com
Rosine d'Uzès, dame du Caylar
French: Rosine d'Uzès, Dame du Caylar
Also Known As:	"Rosine", "Roscie", "Rose"
Birthdate:	1128
Birthplace:	Cailar,Gard,France
Death:	circa 1198 (65-74)
France
Immediate Family:	
Daughter of Rainier d'Uzès, seigneur d'Uzès et de Castellar and Beatrice du Caylar
Wife of Rostaing III de Sabran, coseigneur d'Uzès

Mother of
 Elzéar de Sabran, coseigneur d'Uzès; 
Marta Of Sabran; 
Sabine Of Sabran and 
Rénier de Sabran, seigneur de Caylar

Sister of Guillaume-Raymond du Caylar; N2 du Caylar; Rainon du Caylar; Rostaing du Caylar and N. du Caylar
Occupation:	Dame d'Uzes

sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - ROSCIE, daughter of RAINON Seigneur d’Uzès et de Caylar & his wife Beatrix --- (-before 1206).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Dame Roscie du Caylar於1128年出生在法國奧其坦尼賀勞爾特勒 卡易拉爾時，她的父親Rainier D'UZES38歲，母親Béatrix DU CAYLAR39歲。她於1153年與Rostain II DE SABRAN在法國藍多-盧希隆加爾薩布朗結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1206年在法國奧其坦尼加爾武茲斯享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dame Roscie du Caylar于1128年出生在法国奧其坦尼賀勞爾特勒 卡易拉爾时，她的父亲Rainier D'UZES38岁，母亲Béatrix DU CAYLAR39岁。她于1153年与Rostain II DE SABRAN在法国藍多-盧希隆加爾薩布朗结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1206年在法国奧其坦尼加爾武茲斯享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Caylar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Keillor  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of German Köhler (see Kohler ).

variants.name: Keillor
meanings: Scottish (Perthshire and Angus): habitational name from a place in Angus called Keilor. The placename is perhaps from Gaelic coille or Pictish cell- + a collective or placename forming suffix -or.

nameMeanings.name: Roscie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from a place in northern England named with Old Norse rá ‘roe deer’ + skógr ‘wood, copse’.

en.lifeSummary: When Dame Roscie du Caylar was born in 1128, in Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, France, her father, Rainier D'UZES, was 38 and her mother, Béatrix DU CAYLAR, was 39. She married Rostain II DE SABRAN in 1153, in Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1206, in Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, France, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dame Roscie du Caylar est née en 1128, à Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, France, son père, Rainier D'UZES, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Béatrix DU CAYLAR, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Rostain II DE SABRAN en 1153, à Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1206, à Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, France, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dame Roscie du Caylar 1128 in Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rainier D'UZES 38 und ihre Mutter Béatrix DU CAYLAR 39. Sie hat 1153 in Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Frankreich Rostain II DE SABRAN geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1206 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dame Roscie du Caylar è nata il nel 1128 nel Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, Francia, sua padre, Rainier D'UZES, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Béatrix DU CAYLAR, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Rostain II DE SABRAN nel 1153, nel Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1206, nel Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, Francia, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Dame Roscie du Caylarが1128年にフランスオクシタニーheラウルトル ケイラーで生まれた時，父Rainier D'UZESは38歳，母Béatrix DU CAYLARは39歳であった。1153年にフランスラングエドク-ロウシロンガードサブランでRostain II DE SABRANと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1206年にフランスオクシタニーガードウズeスで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dame Roscie du Caylar(이)가 1128년 프랑스 옥시탠이 he라트 일 카이라에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rainier D'UZES(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Béatrix DU CAYLAR(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1153년 프랑스 랭도c-루시런 가드 사란에서 Rostain II DE SABRAN(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1206년 프랑스 옥시탠이 가드 즈e스에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dame Roscie du Caylar nasceu em 1128, em Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, França, seu pai, Rainier D'UZES, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Béatrix DU CAYLAR, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Rostain II DE SABRAN em 1153, em Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1206, em Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, França, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dame Roscie du Caylar родилась в 1128 г. в местности л кеилар, хeраулт, окситани, Франция, её отцу по имени Rainier D'UZES было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Béatrix DU CAYLAR, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rostain II DE SABRAN в 1153 г. в местности сабран, гард, лангуедок-руссиллон, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1206 г. в местности узeс, гард, окситани, Франция в возрасте 78 лет.

sm.aux environs de 1142 et 1145: mai le 1142 e o'o I le 1145

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dame Roscie du Caylar nació en 1128, en Le Caylar, Hérault, Occitanie, Francia, su papá, Rainier D'UZES, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Béatrix DU CAYLAR, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Rostain II DE SABRAN en 1153, en Sabran, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1206, en Uzès, Gard, Occitanie, Francia, a la edad de 78 años.
